stopConditions: Stop after verified official UNR AI policy/guidance sources and adjacent academic standards have enough evidence for narrow staged claims.
Do not use news announcements, vendor pages, or search snippets as claim evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unr.edu generative AI policy students academic integrity University of Nevada Reno
methodsAttempted.notes: Found OIT AI policy, academic standards, PACK AI technology usage, and academic integrity pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unr.edu artificial intelligence generative AI teaching guidance University of Nevada Reno
methodsAttempted.notes: Found teaching guidance, syllabus guidance, PACK AI technology usage, and Nevada Today launch story.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unr.edu ChatGPT artificial intelligence citation library University of Nevada Reno
methodsAttempted.notes: Found official UNR Library APA generative AI citation guide and student research guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unr.edu AI tools privacy security University of Nevada Reno
methodsAttempted.notes: Found OIT AI policy, technology usage guidance, and a library AI page.
Direct official HTTP fetches returned 200 for selected UNR pages; robots.txt allowed crawling.

artifacts.summary: Official UNR sources include an OIT AI usage policy, PACK AI technology usage guidelines, academic standards that expressly define unauthorized generative AI use as cheating, teaching guidance on academic integrity and AI, syllabus statement guidance, and a UNR Library APA guide for generative AI. The PACK AI launch story was official but rejected as news/event evidence.

artifacts.claimText: UNR's OIT AI usage policy applies to students, employees, contractors, and third parties using AI platforms on behalf of the University, and frames responsible AI use around information security, data privacy, copyright, and academic integrity.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Usage Policy | Office of Information Technology | University of Nevada, Reno
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNR strongly advises users not to share sensitive, confidential, or regulated data with AI tools, because tool data-sharing practices may compromise confidentiality.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Before integrating an AI tool into daily tasks, users should initiate a vendor risk management assessment through the Office of Information Technology’s (OIT) Compliance team. artifacts.sourceTitle: 6,502: Academic Standards | University of Nevada, Reno
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: For this policy, “Cheating” is defined as: (1) Obtaining or providing unauthorized resources and/or information while executing, completing or in relation to coursework ... (2) Unauthorized use of generative artificial intelligence (AI) content generators or generative AI tools.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This includes communicating with students about what is and is not allowed regarding the use of AI in classes and for course assignments. We recommend that you include an AI statement in the syllabus and discuss it with your students on the first day of class.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI - APA Citation Guide (7th Edition) - Library Guides at University of Nevada, Reno
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Like traditional sources, AI-generated works must be cited when used as a source that you are quoting, paraphrasing, or otherwise incorporating. When using generative AI tools in your process (e.g., brainstorming, outlining, proofreading, etc.), do not cite but do acknowledge your use somewhere in your methods section, introduction, or footnote.

artifacts.notes: Narrowly states the policy scope and themes; does not convert all guidance into binding public conclusions beyond the cited page.
Uses the source's strongly advised wording and avoids overstating it as an absolute prohibition.
Supported by official PACK AI guidelines; phrased as a directed should/initiate step rather than an approval claim.
Directly cites the Academic Standards definition of cheating and preserves the unauthorized-use condition.
Treats the page as teaching/academic integrity guidance and does not turn it into a separate disciplinary rule.
Uses the source's recommend language and scopes the claim to instructor syllabus guidance.
Scoped to UNR Library APA citation guidance and not represented as a universal conduct policy.

artifacts.title: University of Nevada, Reno AI Policy and Guidance Candidates
artifacts.summary: Staged candidates for University of Nevada, Reno cover an official OIT AI usage policy, PACK AI technology usage guidelines, academic standards defining unauthorized generative AI use as cheating, teaching guidance on academic integrity and AI detection, syllabus guidance for communicating AI expectations, and UNR Library APA citation guidance for generative AI.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
PACK AI launch news was official but rejected as news/event-only evidence.
The staged claims are agent-reviewed candidates and are not canonical public claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
